content.text: Self-Service Eval Runner and Benchmark Tools
Evaluation should not be gated on a platform engineer's availability. A

lets any team submit an eval job — specifying their test case set, the Claude model to target, and a pass/fail threshold — and receive scored results and a Slack notification when the job completes. The platform maintains a shared
of cross-service test suites and historical baselines so teams can track quality regressions over time and compare models before upgrading.

attrs.alt: Eval runner: developer → API → SQS → Fargate workers → S3/DynamoDB results store → dashboard

content.text: All eval cases use the same JSONL format. Each line is a JSON object with an input, the expected output or grading criteria, and optional metadata.
field selects the scoring method. The runner ships four built-in graders:
Score 1.0 if model output exactly equals expected, else 0.0
Parses both as floats; score 1.0 if within 0.001 tolerance
Score 1.0 if all strings in the list appear in the output
Calls claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 to score 0–10 on the rubric; normalizes to 0–1

# Submit an eval job
acme eval run \
  --cases evals/cases/seed.jsonl \
  --model claude-sonnet-5 \
  --pass-threshold 0.85 \
  --tag release-candidate-v2.3

# Output:
# ✓ Job submitted: eval-job-a3f7c9
# ✓ 42 cases queued
# Estimated completion: ~4 min
# Results will be posted to #eng-invoice-extractor on Slack

# Check job status
acme eval status eval-job-a3f7c9

# Download full results (per-case scores)
acme eval results eval-job-a3f7c9 --output results.json

# Compare two runs
acme eval compare eval-job-a3f7c9 eval-job-b2d4e1

# List historical runs for your service
acme eval history --service invoice-extractor --last 10

# eval_service/api.py
import uuid, json, boto3
from fastapi import FastAPI, Depends, HTTPException
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from acme_auth.fastapi import get_current_user_claims

app = FastAPI(title="Eval Runner Service")
_sqs = boto3.client("sqs")
_ddb = boto3.resource("dynamodb").Table("eval-jobs")
QUEUE_URL = "https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789/eval-jobs"


class EvalRunRequest(BaseModel):
    service_name: str
    cases_s3_path: str = Field(..., description="s3://bucket/path/to/cases.jsonl")
    model: str = "claude-sonnet-5"
    pass_threshold: float = Field(0.80, ge=0.0, le=1.0)
    max_concurrency: int = Field(10, ge=1, le=50)
    tag: str = ""


@app.post("/eval/run")
async def submit_eval(
    req: EvalRunRequest,
    claims: dict = Depends(get_current_user_claims),
):
    job_id = f"eval-job-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}"
    # Store job metadata
    _ddb.put_item(Item={
        "job_id": job_id,
        "service_name": req.service_name,
        "model": req.model,
        "pass_threshold": str(req.pass_threshold),
        "cases_s3_path": req.cases_s3_path,
        "status": "queued",
        "submitted_by": claims["email"],
        "tag": req.tag,
    })
    # Enqueue
    _sqs.send_message(
        QueueUrl=QUEUE_URL,
        MessageBody=json.dumps({"job_id": job_id, **req.model_dump()}),
    )
    return {"job_id": job_id, "status": "queued"}


@app.get("/eval/{job_id}")
async def get_status(job_id: str):
    item = _ddb.get_item(Key={"job_id": job_id}).get("Item")
    if not item:
        raise HTTPException(404, "Job not found")
    return item

# eval_service/worker.py  (runs on ECS Fargate, pulls from SQS)
import json, time, boto3, anthropic, concurrent.futures
from .graders import grade_case

_sqs = boto3.client("sqs")
_s3 = boto3.client("s3")
_ddb = boto3.resource("dynamodb").Table("eval-jobs")
QUEUE_URL = "https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789/eval-jobs"


def run_single_case(case: dict, model: str, claude: anthropic.Anthropic) -> dict:
    start = time.monotonic()
    response = claude.messages.create(
        model=model,
        max_tokens=1024,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": case["input"]}],
    )
    output = response.content[0].text
    latency_ms = round((time.monotonic() - start) * 1000)
    score = grade_case(case, output)
    return {
        "input": case["input"],
        "output": output,
        "score": score,
        "latency_ms": latency_ms,
        "input_tokens": response.usage.input_tokens,
        "output_tokens": response.usage.output_tokens,
    }


def process_job(job: dict) -> None:
    job_id = job["job_id"]
    model = job["model"]
    pass_threshold = float(job["pass_threshold"])
    max_concurrency = int(job.get("max_concurrency", 10))

    # Load cases from S3
    bucket, key = job["cases_s3_path"].replace("s3://", "").split("/", 1)
    cases = [json.loads(l) for l in _s3.get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=key)["Body"]
             .read().decode().splitlines() if l.strip()]

    _ddb.update_item(Key={"job_id": job_id},
                     UpdateExpression="SET #s = :s, total_cases = :n",
                     ExpressionAttributeNames={"#s": "status"},
                     ExpressionAttributeValues={":s": "running", ":n": len(cases)})

    claude = anthropic.Anthropic()
    results = []
    with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=max_concurrency) as pool:
        futures = [pool.submit(run_single_case, c, model, claude) for c in cases]
        for future in concurrent.futures.as_completed(futures):
            results.append(future.result())

    avg_score = sum(r["score"] for r in results) / len(results) if results else 0.0
    passed = avg_score >= pass_threshold

    # Store results
    _s3.put_object(
        Bucket=bucket,
        Key=f"eval-results/{job_id}/results.json",
        Body=json.dumps({"job_id": job_id, "results": results, "avg_score": avg_score, "passed": passed}),
    )
    _ddb.update_item(Key={"job_id": job_id},
                     UpdateExpression="SET #s = :s, avg_score = :sc, passed = :p",
                     ExpressionAttributeNames={"#s": "status"},
                     ExpressionAttributeValues={":s": "complete", ":sc": str(avg_score), ":p": passed})

The benchmark registry is a shared collection of curated test suites maintained by the platform team. Teams can run their service against a standard suite to compare their performance against the fleet baseline, or to validate behavior before a model upgrade.

# List available benchmark suites
acme eval benchmarks list

# Output:
# ┌─────────────────────────┬──────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ Suite                   │ Cases        │ Description                          │
# ├─────────────────────────┼──────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┤
# │ claude-extraction-v1    │ 200 cases    │ Structured data extraction from text │
# │ claude-classification-v1│ 150 cases    │ Multi-class text classification      │
# │ claude-summarization-v1 │ 100 cases    │ Abstractive summarization quality    │
# │ claude-code-gen-v1      │ 80 cases     │ Python function generation & tests   │
# │ claude-rag-qa-v1        │ 120 cases    │ RAG answer quality with citations    │
# └─────────────────────────┴──────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────┘

# Run your service against the extraction benchmark
acme eval run \
  --benchmark claude-extraction-v1 \
  --model claude-sonnet-5 \
  --pass-threshold 0.85

# Compare two models on the same benchmark
acme eval run --benchmark claude-extraction-v1 --model claude-sonnet-5 --tag baseline
acme eval run --benchmark claude-extraction-v1 --model claude-opus-4-8 --tag candidate
acme eval compare --tag baseline --tag candidate

The eval runner integrates with CI as a quality gate. A failed eval blocks deployment — the same as a failing unit test. The platform provides a reusable GitHub Actions action that teams include in their workflow:

# .github/workflows/ci.yml
jobs:
  evals:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Upload eval cases to S3
        run: |
          aws s3 cp evals/cases/seed.jsonl \
            s3://acme-eval-inputs/${{ github.repository }}/${{ github.sha }}/cases.jsonl

      - name: Submit eval job
        id: eval
        uses: acme-org/eval-runner-action@v2
        with:
          service-name: ${{ env.SERVICE_NAME }}
          cases-s3-path: s3://acme-eval-inputs/${{ github.repository }}/${{ github.sha }}/cases.jsonl
          model: claude-sonnet-5
          pass-threshold: "0.85"
          timeout-minutes: "10"

      - name: Fail on low eval score
        if: steps.eval.outputs.passed == 'false'
        run: |
          echo "Eval FAILED: avg_score=${{ steps.eval.outputs.avg_score }} < 0.85 threshold"
          exit 1
(or vice versa), teams run a comparison eval to quantify the quality and cost trade-off:

# Side-by-side model comparison
acme eval run --cases evals/cases/seed.jsonl --model claude-sonnet-5  --tag sonnet
acme eval run --cases evals/cases/seed.jsonl --model claude-opus-4-8  --tag opus
acme eval run --cases evals/cases/seed.jsonl --model claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 --tag haiku

acme eval compare --tags sonnet,opus,haiku

# Output:
# Model                  Avg Score    Cost/run    p50 latency    Recommendation
# claude-sonnet-5        0.883        $0.42       1.2s           ✓ current
# claude-opus-4-8        0.921        $2.10       3.1s           upgrade if budget allows
# claude-haiku-4-5-20251001  0.791    $0.04       0.4s           consider for high-volume
The self-service eval runner lets teams submit jobs via CLI or portal, specifying their JSONL case file, target model, and pass threshold — no platform engineer involvement required.

Five built-in graders (exact match, case-insensitive, number tolerance, contains-all, LLM judge) cover the majority of evaluation tasks; the LLM judge grader uses claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 for cost efficiency.
A shared benchmark registry of curated test suites lets teams run standardized evals and track their scores against historical baselines and cross-service fleet averages.
CI integration via a reusable GitHub Actions action blocks deployment when the eval average score falls below the configured threshold — treating quality regression as a build failure.
Model comparison runs (same cases, multiple models) give teams a quantitative quality-versus-cost trade-off table before committing to a model upgrade.
